reasoning: The Polymarket probability was around 15% on late February 2025. There are three primary pathways to a nuclear detonation in 2025: a North Korean test, an intentional US test (and subsequent chain reaction by Russia/China), or tactical weapon use in a conflict like Ukraine. North Korea stringently remains the most prominent threat; South Korean intelligence has highlighted that Pyongyang has "completed preparations" for its seventh test at Tunnel No. 3, a status maintained roughly since 2022 without action, suggesting Kim Jong Un is waiting for the optimal political moment. Domestically in the US, while Trump's allies and Project 2025 have strongly advocated for a resumption of explosive testing, technical constraints exist. The Nevada National Security Site (NNSS) formally mandates a 36-month readiness timeline, though reports suggest an expedited simple test could theoretically take roughly six months. Even if Trump ordered a test immediately, completing it within the 2025 calendar year would be logistically difficult, pushing potential US tests (and subsequent Russian responses) to 2026 or later. Without imminent concrete signals of a US order or North Korean action, a 16% probability aligns well with the structural readiness and the small but non-zero tail risks of geopolitical escalation.
